@graph.name: IVF Treatment (In Vitro Fertilization) — Bavishi Fertility Institute

@graph.followup: A Beta-HCG blood test is performed about 13–15 days after embryo transfer to confirm pregnancy.
@graph.howPerformed: Eggs are retrieved from the ovaries after controlled stimulation, fertilised with sperm in the laboratory (usually by ICSI), cultured into embryos, and the best embryo is transferred into the uterus.
@graph.name: IVF Treatment

acceptedAnswer.text: IVF is an assisted-reproduction technique in which eggs are retrieved from the ovaries, fertilised by sperm in a laboratory, and the resulting embryo is transferred into the uterus to achieve pregnancy.
Generally about one year of trying if the woman is under 35, or six months if she is 35 or older. With known fertility issues — or if the woman is over 40 — it is wise to consult a specialist sooner.

mainEntity.name: How long should a couple try before considering IVF?

acceptedAnswer.text: In conventional IVF, eggs and sperm are mixed in a dish. In ICSI, a single healthy sperm is injected directly into each egg. ICSI is preferred for male-factor infertility or previous fertilisation failure — at Bavishi Fertility Institute we use ICSI for all couples.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between IVF and ICSI?

acceptedAnswer.text: Age is the single biggest factor — younger women generally have higher success rates. For older patients, options such as donor eggs, previously-frozen embryos or advanced IVF techniques can improve the chances.

mainEntity.name: How does age affect IVF success?

acceptedAnswer.text: From the start of stimulation to embryo transfer, the active treatment usually takes about 12–17 days, plus the pre-treatment evaluation beforehand.

mainEntity.name: How long does one IVF cycle take?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Egg retrieval is a short, painless procedure done through the vagina under light sedation — with no cut and no stitch. Most patients go home within about two hours.

Cost depends on your diagnosis, the protocol and any add-ons such as ICSI, PGT or donor programmes. Bavishi Fertility Institute offers transparent pricing with no hidden costs, easy / interest-free EMI and the Suraksha Kavach package. Book a consultation for a personalised estimate.

mainEntity.name: How much does IVF cost at Bavishi Fertility Institute?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — through the Suraksha Kavach programme, which provides financial protection and assurance on your fertility journey. Speak to our team to see if you qualify.

mainEntity.name: Does Bavishi Fertility Institute offer a money-back guarantee for IVF?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bavishi Fertility Institute is one of India's most trusted IVF chains — operating since 1984 across 15 centres in 8 cities, with 30,000+ successful pregnancies and the National Fertility Award for five consecutive years.

mainEntity.name: Which is the best IVF centre in India?

acceptedAnswer.text: Focus on a healthy lifestyle — a balanced diet, regular exercise, maintaining a healthy weight, and avoiding smoking and excessive alcohol. Your specialist will give you any additional, personalised advice during your consultation.

mainEntity.name: What lifestyle changes should I make before starting IVF?

acceptedAnswer.text: There are no strict restrictions, but a balanced diet rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains and lean proteins is recommended, along with good hydration. Follow any specific guidance your clinic provides for your treatment.

mainEntity.name: Are there any dietary recommendations during IVF?

acceptedAnswer.text: Embryo grading assesses the quality of embryos based on their appearance and development. Higher-grade embryos are more likely to implant successfully, which is why our embryologists select the best-quality embryo(s) for transfer.
Yes. IVF can be used by single individuals — with sperm collection for men, or donor sperm for women — and, where needed, a gestational carrier. Our team will explain the options available to you.

mainEntity.name: Can single individuals have a baby through IVF?
